feat: Glossaire — soulignement pointillé et infobulle latérale pour les termes définis (#59)

Implémente la fonctionnalité glossaire de l'issue CALME : les termes
définis dans un dictionnaire JSON sont soulignés en pointillé
(`text-decoration: underline dotted`) dans le rendu Markdown ; clic →
définition + lien dans le panneau latéral, survol → infobulle après
délai configurable.

## Changements

### Couche pure (`src/core/`)
- **`types.ts`** — interfaces `GlossaryTerm`, `GlossaryConfig`,
`GlossaryMatchingConfig`, `GlossaryUiConfig`
- **`glossary.ts`** — fonctions pures : `buildGlossaryMap`,
`mergeGlossarySources`, `buildTermRegex` (regex Unicode avec flag `u`,
tri longest-first pour éviter les sous-chaînes)

### Adaptateur navigateur (`src/adapters/browser/glossary.ts`)
- Fetch parallèle des sources JSON + merge last-wins par clé
`term.toLowerCase()` / `alias.toLowerCase()`
- Tree-walker DOM sur les nœuds texte ; exclusion configurable (`code`,
`pre`, `kbd`)
- Wrapping en `<span class="ow-term" role="button">` avec gestionnaire
clic → `#ow-glossary-panel` dans `#sidebar`
- Support SVG inline (`<text>/<tspan>`) optionnel via `svg.inline: true`

### Intégration (`src/adapters/browser/enhance.ts`)
```typescript
// Appelé en fin de pipeline afterRender
if (cfg.glossary?.enabled !== false && cfg.glossary?.sources?.length) {
  await applyGlossary(appEl, cfg.glossary)
}
```

### Config (`config.json`)
```json
{
  "glossary": {
    "enabled": true,
    "sources": ["./glossary.fr.json"],
    "matching": { "caseSensitive": false, "wordBoundary": true, "firstOccurrenceOnly": false },
    "exclude": { "elements": ["code", "pre", "kbd"] },
    "ui": { "underlineStyle": "dotted", "clickBehavior": "sidebar", "tooltip": { "showOnHover": true, "delay": 300 } }
  }
}
```

### Démo & tests
- `docs/demos/03-glossary/` — page HTML + `.fr.md` / `.en.md` +
dictionnaires `glossary.{fr,en}.json` avec termes CALME (UAF, AEG,
LGGRI, Capabilité, Gouvernance IT)
- `tests/glossary.test.ts` — 11 tests Vitest sur les fonctions pures
(tous verts)
- `tests/e2e/demos/03-glossary.spec.js` — tests Playwright (annotation
des termes, exclusion `<code>`, clic sidebar, snapshot visuel)

## Checklist
- [x] Convention de commit respectée
- [ ] Lint OK / Type-check OK / Tests OK
- [x] Docs/README mis à jour si nécessaire
- [x] Breaking changes documentées ou aucune
- [x] Tests ajoutés ou N/A
- [x] Portée petite et réversible

## Notes
- Le dist (`dist/ontowave.js`, `docs/ontowave.min.js`) est rebuilté
depuis `src/main.ts` via `vite.config.dist.ts` — la démo utilise le
build local.
- 2 tests E2E encore en échec : (1) les 404 optionnels (`nav.yml`,
`search-index.json`) apparaissent comme console errors — à filtrer via
événements `response` ; (2) le gestionnaire clic du sidebar panel
nécessite une investigation complémentaire du binding de closure dans le
bundle minifié.
- Merge multiple sources : last-wins sur `term.toLowerCase()` ; les
`aliases` sont remplacés intégralement (pas d'union).

commit.message:
feat: Navigation transparente vers fichiers .puml depuis Markdown (#51)

Clicking a Markdown link pointing to a `.puml` file now loads and
renders the diagram inline via Kroki, with full SPA navigation (hash
history, browser back button).

## Changes

**`src/core/logic.ts`**
- `resolvePumlCandidates()` — resolves `.puml` URLs from roots config
(mirrors markdown resolver, keeps extension)
- `rewriteLinksHtml()` — extended to rewrite `.puml` hrefs to hash
routes (extension preserved, unlike `.md` which strips it)

**`src/app.ts`**
- `loadPuml()` — fetches raw `.puml` content via content service
- `renderRoute()` — detects `.puml` suffix → wraps content in `
```plantuml ``` ` block → passes through existing Kroki SVG pipeline

**`src/adapters/browser/router.ts` + `src/router.ts` +
`src/markdown.ts`**
- Click interception extended to `.puml` links; navigate to
`#/path/to/file.puml`

**Demo & tests**
- `docs/demos/01-base/architecture.puml` — sample diagram with `[[url]]`
hyperlinks
- `docs/demos/01-base/puml-navigation.{fr,en}.md` + `.html` — demo page
linking to the `.puml` file
- 9 new unit tests, 1 new E2E spec

```markdown
<!-- In any .md file -->
Voir le [diagramme d'architecture](architecture.puml).
```
→ OntoWave intercepts the click, fetches `architecture.puml`, renders
SVG via Kroki, updates the hash to `#/.../architecture.puml`.

## Checklist
- [x] Convention de commit respectée
- [x] Lint OK / Type-check OK / Tests OK
- [x] Docs/README mis à jour si nécessaire
- [x] Breaking changes documentées ou aucune
- [x] Tests ajoutés ou N/A
- [x] Portée petite et réversible

## Notes
- `.puml` routes keep the extension in the hash (e.g.
`#/demos/arch.puml`) so `renderRoute` can distinguish them from markdown
routes without ambiguity.
- SVG rendering reuses the existing Kroki pipeline (`enhance.ts`) — no
new network dependency.
- `[[url]]` hyperlinks in PlantUML diagrams remain functional in the
rendered SVG.

---------

L'ancienne librairie monolithique (~550KB, API `locales`/`sources`) était toujours servie sur CDN, tandis que toutes les pages de démos utilisaient encore l'ancienne API incompatible. ## Changements ### Build / Distribution - Reconstruit `dist/ontowave.js` + `dist/ontowave.min.js` depuis `src/main.ts` via `vite build --config vite.config.dist.ts` (bundle IIFE, 4.5MB minifié) - Copie automatique vers `docs/ontowave.min.js` dans le script `build:package` - Ajout du script `build:lib` dans `package.json` ### Corrections de bugs dans la librairie - **`view.ts`** : lookup lazy de `#app` (évite crash si script chargé avant le DOM) - **`main.ts`** : redirection initiale utilise `i18n.default` du config (était hardcodé `#index.md`) - **`enhance.ts`** : vérification de délégation de sous-module requiert ≥3 segments de chemin (était ≥2, déclenchait des 404 sur toutes les routes) - **`search.ts`** : un index bundlé vide (`[]`) arrête le fallthrough vers le fetch du sitemap - **`main.ts`** : la fonction `check()` vérifie le bundle avant de faire un `fetch` (supprime les erreurs console 404 pour `/nav.yml` et `/search-index.json`) ### Migration des pages de démos (8 pages) Passage de l'ancienne API à la nouvelle sur toutes les pages `docs/demos/**/*.html` : ```html
``` ### Config & structure - Création de `public/config.json` et `docs/config.json` pour le site principal - Mise à jour de `index.html` (racine) vers entry Vite TypeScript (`src/main.ts`) - Mise à jour de `docs/index.html` vers la nouvelle API - Snapshots E2E supprimés (régénérés automatiquement au prochain run) ## Checklist - [ ] Convention de commit respectée - [ ] Lint OK / Type-check OK / Tests OK - [ ] Docs/README mis à jour si nécessaire - [ ] Breaking changes documentées ou aucune - [ ] Tests ajoutés ou N/A - [ ] Portée petite et réversible ## Notes - Les demos référencent `/ontowave.min.js` (local) pendant cette transition. commit.message:
Add CalendarWidget and DatePickerWidget (#251)

* Add CalendarWidget composing GridWidget for month view layout

Introduces a CalendarWidget that arranges a month view using GridWidget
with 7 columns (one per weekday) and rows for each week. Features:

- Configurable first day of week (default: Sunday)
- Day-of-week header row (toggleable)
- Today highlighting with bracket markers
- Handles leap years and all month layouts correctly
- CalendarTheme for customizable colors
- CalendarExtensions with fluent API (Calendar(), ShowHeader(), etc.)
- CalendarDemo sample registered in apphost.cs
- 20 unit tests covering layout, placement, and edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Evolve CalendarWidget with CalendarNode, focus, selection, and Day builder

CalendarWidget now reconciles to a CalendarNode that wraps an internally-built
GridWidget. The CalendarNode manages focus, selection state, and keyboard
navigation while the Grid handles layout and rendering.

Key changes:
- CalendarNode: focusable node wrapping Grid child, arrow key navigation
  (L/R ±1 day, U/D ±7 days), SelectedDay state with Enter/Space to select
- CalendarDayContext: record providing day info (Date, IsToday, IsSelected,
  IsWeekend, DayOfWeek) to the Day content builder callback
- CalendarDateSelectedEventArgs: event args for OnSelected handler
- Default mode renders with gridlines; Compact() removes them
- Today highlighted with ANSI reverse video (\x1b[7m)
- Selected day also uses reverse video
- Day(callback) allows custom content per day cell (HStack layout)
- OnSelected(handler) fires when Enter/Space pressed on a day
- CalendarTheme updated with Selected fg/bg colors
- 42 unit + integration tests covering layout, navigation, events
- CalendarDemo updated with interactive selection display

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update CalendarDemo with TabPanel showing Compact, Bordered, and Day Content modes

Three tabs demonstrate each calendar presentation:
- Compact: no gridlines, tight layout for DatePicker embedding
- Bordered: default mode with gridlines around each day cell
- Day Content: bordered with Day() builder adding emoji to specific days

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Enable mouse support in CalendarDemo

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Make calendar day cells interactive via InteractableWidget

Each day cell is now wrapped in an InteractableWidget, making it
individually focusable, clickable, and hoverable. CalendarNode no
longer manages focus/keyboard navigation itself — it delegates to
the grid's child InteractableNodes.

Key changes:
- Day cells wrapped in InteractableWidget with OnClick handlers
- CalendarNode.IsFocusable = false (cells are focusable)
- CalendarNode.GetFocusableNodes() delegates to child grid
- Removed CalendarNode keyboard bindings (Enter/Space handled by
  InteractableWidget; arrow nav can be re-added as container concern)
- BuildDayText uses ic.IsFocused for visual feedback
- Removed unused ActionIds from CalendarWidget

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix TabPanel mouse clicks: remove .Selected() that overrides user selection

The .Selected() call on the first tab made it a 'controlled' component,
forcing SelectedIndex=0 on every re-render. This overrode any tab switch
triggered by mouse click or keyboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix calendar grid sizing: uniform cells, skip Day builder in compact mode

- Compact mode: skip DayBuilder callback, use Content sizing (minimal)
- Non-compact mode: use SizeHint.Fill for columns AND rows so all cells
  are uniform regardless of Day builder content
- Added tests for compact/non-compact sizing and Day builder suppression

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move arrow key bindings to individual cells for reliable navigation

Arrow key bindings on parent containers (BorderWidget, VStackWidget,
GridWidget) did not reliably fire when the focused node was a child
InteractableWidget. By placing the arrow key WithInputBindings directly
on each InteractableWidget cell, the bindings are checked first during
input routing since they are on the focused node itself.

This affects:
- DatePicker year grid cells (4-column navigation)
- DatePicker month grid cells (4-column navigation)
- Calendar day cells (7-column navigation)

PageUp/PageDown bindings remain on the parent BorderWidget since they
don't depend on focus-relative navigation.

Added integration test for arrow key navigation with surrounding widgets
(ToggleSwitch + DatePicker) to verify bindings work in realistic layouts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add visual focus indicator for keyboard navigation

The arrow key navigation logic was working correctly all along — the
diagnostic integration test proves focus moves through year→month→calendar
grids via arrow keys and selects the correct date.

The real issue was that IsCellFocused was tracked but never used in
rendering, so there was NO VISUAL FEEDBACK when focus moved via keyboard.
The user would press arrow keys, focus would move internally, but
nothing changed on screen.

Fix: Add IsCellFocused to the hover color condition in both
DatePickerCellNode and CalendarDayNode. Focused cells now show the same
hover-style highlight (gray background), making keyboard navigation
visible. This also provides a subtle starting-point indicator when the
popup first opens and auto-focus lands on the first cell.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add smart focus targeting and Tab dismiss for DatePicker

- Year grid: focus on selected year or current year
- Month grid: focus on selected month (if viewing selected year) or
  current month (if viewing current year)
- Calendar step: focus on selected day or current day via new
  CalendarWidget.FocusDay property
- Tab/Shift-Tab on any picker cell dismisses popup and moves focus to
  the next/previous sibling widget
- Added CalendarWidget.CellTabBindings for custom tab overrides
- Added InteractableWidget.RequestFocus for programmatic initial focus

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Navigate to prev/next year page at grid edges

Left arrow on the leftmost column triggers PageYearsBackward;
right arrow on the rightmost column triggers PageYearsForward.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Preserve focus row on year page transitions

When navigating off the left edge, focus lands on the rightmost column
of the same row on the previous page. Navigating off the right edge
lands on the leftmost column of the same row on the next page.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix year grid navigation: RequestFocus, popup focus, and edge paging

Three bugs fixed:

1. RequestFocus was being overridden by ZStackWidget popup focus
   management which clears all focus then sets first child. Now
   preserves any existing RequestFocus target in the popup layer.

2. RequestFocus re-applied every render frame, overriding user-driven
   focus changes from arrow keys. Added HasAppliedRequestFocus flag
   on InteractableNode so focus is only set once per target.

3. Edge navigation test added: Left from leftmost column pages
   backward with focus on rightmost column same row, Right from
   rightmost column pages forward with focus on leftmost column.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix year picker page-transition focus: clear stale IsFocused before rebuild

When pressing LEFT at col 0 or RIGHT at col 3 to trigger a year page
transition, the departing cell retained IsFocused=true because the
RequestFocus reconciliation path (node.IsFocused = true) never clears
focus on other nodes. This created a dual-focus state where both the
old cell (index 4) and the new target cell (index 7) had IsFocused=true.

While FocusRing.Rebuild's safety-net resolves this by keeping the last
focused node, the transient dual-focus could cause visual flicker and
was fragile. The proper fix is to explicitly clear IsFocused on the
departing cell in the edge handlers before triggering the page change,
so the rebuilt grid has exactly one focused node.

Adds regression tests for both LEFT-edge (page backward) and
RIGHT-edge (page forward) transitions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix year/month grid vertical nav and add ESC to dismiss picker

Vertical navigation (UP/DOWN) in the year and month grids now clamps
at the grid edges instead of escaping into the prev/next icon nodes.
The icons are focusable (IsFocusable => IsClickable), so the flat
focusables list included them after the 12 grid cells, and a DOWN
from the bottom row would navigate into the icon area.

ESC now dismisses the popup from any picker step (year, month, or
calendar day) instead of only going back one step. The previous
behavior required multiple ESC presses and had no ESC binding at
all on the year step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restore focus to trigger button on dismiss, add down-arrow to open picker

When ESC/Backspace dismisses the popup, focus is now restored to the
trigger button via PopupStack.Pop(out focusRestoreNode). Previously
Pop() discarded the restore node, leaving focus unset.

Down arrow on the focused trigger button now opens the picker popup,
matching standard combobox/picker keyboard UX.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Calendar compact mode tests: header uses single-char abbreviations

The compact calendar's narrow columns (2-3 chars) only fit single-letter
day-of-week headers ('S','M','T') via CalendarHeaderNode's responsive
format selection. Tests were waiting for 'Su' (two-letter format) which
requires 3+ char columns, causing WaitUntil timeouts.

Fixed to wait for day numbers instead of header format, which is stable
regardless of column width.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(maglev): prevent repeated callee Maglev deopt reload cycle

After a callee's Maglev code deopts in call_undefined_receiver0_inner,
the Rc cache was cleared but the Arc storage was not. Each subsequent
call would reload Maglev from Arc via lazy transfer, execute it, deopt
again, and repeat — causing O(N) deopt cycles for N closure calls.

Fix: (1) Guard the entire Maglev section in call_undefined_receiver0_inner
with !ba.jit_maglev_has_deopted(), matching call_js_function's pattern.
(2) Clear both Rc and Arc storage on callee deopt so lazy transfer cannot
reload deopted code. (3) Move lazy transfer inside the deopt guard in
call_js_function to avoid wasteful Arc loads.

This fixes the closure_counter 5x regression introduced by the callee
deopt propagation fix (5351b3b8).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
